Label: N/A
Issue: The document does not specify the total number of supported concurrent users.
Suggestion: The system shall handle up to 10,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.

Label: Section 3.1
Issue: Does not specify what happens if the telemetry data acquisition fails.
Suggestion: If telemetry data acquisition fails, the system shall retry up to three times before alerting the administrator.

Label: APAF-FR-01
Issue: Does not specify the frequency of data acquisition from ESOC.
Suggestion: The APAF system shall acquire telemetry data from ESOC every 24 hours.

Label: APAF-FR-02
Issue: Does not specify the format of the IDFS data sets.
Suggestion: The IDFS data sets shall be in a format compliant with the NASA Planetary Data System (PDS) standards.

Label: APAF-FR-07
Issue: Does not specify the update frequency for web-based displays.
Suggestion: Web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data shall be updated every 15 minutes.

Label: APAF-PR-01
Issue: Does not specify the password policy for the web server.
Suggestion: The APAF system web server shall enforce a strong password policy, requiring at least 12 characters, including uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.

Label: APAF-CR-04
Issue: Does not specify the bandwidth requirements for the network connection between SwRI and NISN.
Suggestion: The network connection between SwRI and NISN shall have a minimum bandwidth of 100 Mbps.

Label: APAF-LR-01
Issue: Does not specify the frequency of system maintenance.
Suggestion: SwRI shall provide APAF system maintenance on a quarterly basis.

Label: APAF-DR-01a
Issue: Does not specify the actions to be taken if the transmission and processing are not error-free.
Suggestion: If the transmission and processing are not error-free, the system shall log the errors and notify the administrator for manual intervention.

Label: APAF-DR-02c
Issue: Does not specify the format for providing data to NASA PDS.
Suggestion: ASPERA-3 data shall be provided to NASA PDS in a PDS-compliant format, including metadata and documentation.

Label: Section 3.9.4
Issue: Does not specify any software quality metrics.
Suggestion: The APAF data system shall adhere to software quality metrics including reliability (99.9% uptime), maintainability (mean time to repair of 4 hours), and usability (user satisfaction score of 85%).

Label: Section 3.9.7
Issue: Does not specify any training requirements for users and operators.
Suggestion: The APAF Operations Procedures Document shall include detailed training materials and guidelines for users and operators.
